assignees: Ethan Vandenberg, Keith Bruss, Predrag, Savana
description:
These plugins are vulnerable on the site.


  Recommended Action                                             
                   
  1. Deactivate and delete PAFE free + PAFE Pro immediately — no content depends on them. you can do this now.


2. For JetSmartFilters: Before removing, you need to edit the two templates in Elementor:


This plugin is vulnerable and also in use on the website.  It is used to add filters to the blog post and video archive posts:

https://www.fhvlegal.com/blog/

https://www.fhvlegal.com/video/
Our suggestion is to remove this immediately:

    - Open the Blog Feed template (Elementor Library → post 1062) and remove/replace the checkboxes filter widget                                                                                       
    - Open the Video Archive template (post 1364) and remove/replace the search filter widget               

Once those widgets are removed or replaced, JetSmartFilters can be safely deactivated and deleted. 

Alternatively somebody can purchase a license for JetSmartFilters at $43/year so we can continue to receive updates to this plugin. https://crocoblock.com/plugins/jetsmartfilters/

We can also look into replacing the functionality with a free or developed solution.

title: Include Sitemap In footer of Client websites
description:
Hi, this is inline w/ a group of tasks we have from a sheet. Here is the link for reference: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1qPfUUC1J2r1s7Ot8Y1PmIcje6WpyMszDhxHGz4TxDTk/edit?gid=0#gid=0

SEO added more info column D. 

I have included the task info here:
--- 
Hey Team!

To enhance our technical SEO baseline, improve crawl efficiency, and ensure consistent signaling across all client properties, It would be great to come to an agreement to outlining a new, standardized protocol for crawl management.

My suggestion is as follows:

Implement the following three directives on all client websites as part of our standard build and maintenance process.

1. XML Sitemap in robots.txt (Primary Directive)

This is the most effective way to inform search engines of all indexable URLs.

Action: Ensure the robots.txt file for every client domain contains a Sitemap directive pointing to the full URL of the XML sitemap.

Implementation Example:
User-agent: 
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap.xml

The "Why": This provides a clear, direct path for Google to find and crawl our most important pages, ensuring faster discovery and indexing of new content.

2. User-Facing HTML Sitemap (Footer)

This is for user experience and secondary crawl pathing.

Action: All client websites must have a user-facing HTML sitemap.

Implementation: This sitemap must be linked in the footer of every page on the site.

The "Why": This provides a clear navigation path for users, improves site accessibility, and   acts as a secondary crawl path for search engines to discover deep pages.

Strategic Crawl & Indexation Control

This is critical. We must be precise in telling crawlers what to crawl and what to index.

A. To Control Crawling (Using robots.txt)

We must Disallow crawlers from accessing low-value, non-SEO pages to conserve crawl budget.

Action: Audit and update the robots.txt file to Disallow non-public or utility sections of the site.
Implementation Examples:
Disallow: /wp-admin/

The WP sitemap page  plugin in perfect to use as a plugin for now

Regarding providing a robot.txt:

They differ per client especially regarding disallows but here is a comprehensive one that can be edited according to client needs especially regarding disallows:

User-agent: *

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap_index.xml

Also, just an example of placement. Sitemap listed at the very bottom of the footer on the left followed by privacy policy etc.

* In the footer we will use the page /sitemap/ and not the XML sitemap.  The XML sitemap is for Google & the robots.txt.

title: FHV Legal Reporting Call Notes
description:
Ethan, please review FHV Legal's reporting call and complete adding the call notes to our reporting call section.
If any work needs to be tasked out from the call, confirm with clayton that he hasn't already assigned it out to his team. I just don't want to copy and paste this blindly as I didn't get to attend the call.

Thanks!

Fireflies AI Transcript:
https://app.fireflies.ai/view/FHV-Legal-Reporting-Call::01JT3WACB5KRG30T01PJKYTFP7
 
Zoom notes: Meeting summary for FHV Legal Reporting Call (05/07/2025)
Quick recap
The meeting focused on SEO strategies and website optimization for a law firm specializing in medical malpractice. The team discussed various tools and techniques to improve their online visibility, including keyword targeting, geolocation strategies, and website improvements. They also addressed the importance of consistent branding, digital PR campaigns, and future planning for office locations and social media content creation.

Next steps
Clayton to implement changes to the website's main menu and footer for optimization.
Clayton to reorganize and consolidate video pages created by CAMG to improve SEO.
Corey to continue with the Digital PR campaign, moving into the data validation stage and preparing to pitch to media outlets.
Corey to follow up with agency contacts regarding logo and print materials redesign for the firm.
Michael to coordinate with Corey on updating the firm's signature block and letterhead design.
Click.See team to continue building citation networks and improving backlink profile.
Click.See team to prepare for creating satellite location pages on the website.
Ray and Daniel to be ready for potential requests to set up new office locations for SEO purposes.
Click.See team to incorporate Ray and Daniel's social media campaign content into their SEO strategy.

SummarySEO Data-Driven Project Progress
In the meeting, Clayton, the president of SEO at Click, discussed the progress of their data-driven project. He mentioned that they had spent the first couple of months conducting full audits on the website. Clayton also introduced the Geo grids, which are specific search queries that help determine where Google sees them. He explained that each node represents a different search query and that the full grid size is about 12 miles. Clayton also mentioned that they are only in the early stages of month two and proposed changes to the list of keywords for Maps visibility. He asked for feedback on the specific terms that should be tracked in Maps.

Keyword Strategy for Medical Malpractice Services
In the meeting, Ray, Clayton, and Danny discussed the importance of using specific keywords for their medical malpractice services. They agreed that terms like "medical malpractice attorney" and "medical malpractice lawyers" are crucial. Clayton introduced a new tool, a rank tracker, which provides data-driven insights into their search visibility. The tool shows their ranking in both maps and organic search for various keywords, including "medical malpractice attorney" and "misdiagnosis attorneys." They also discussed the tool's ability to track geographic-specific searches within Fort Lauderdale, Florida. The team agreed to continue refining their keyword strategy based on the data provided by the rank tracker.

Improving Map Visibility and Organic Growth
Clayton discussed the company's performance in organic and map areas, noting that while they are growing in specific areas, they need to improve map visibility. Daniel asked about the reason for a small green area in the map, to which Clayton explained that Google is testing their business profile in specific nodes to determine relevance. Ray suggested that the company's plan is to target a small percentage of the population in South Florida, but Clayton clarified that the map area they are discussing does not include the West population.

Expanding Geogrid for South Florida
Clayton discussed the importance of increasing visibility in South Florida, particularly in areas with low visibility. He proposed expanding the geogrid to cover the entire region. Ray and Daniel emphasized the need for the firm to be the first choice for clients, suggesting that achieving this would lead to better case quality. Corey confirmed that the current strategy aligns with their goals. The team expressed readiness to accelerate their efforts, including discussing satellite locations and creating subpages for each city.

Website Optimization for Business Profile
In the meeting, Clayton discussed the foundation elements of their business profile, emphasizing the importance of a strong website for expanding visibility. He mentioned that they have cleaned up and optimized their website, which will help carry over authority to their business profile. Clayton also mentioned that they are building satellite location pages and will soon start mapping out specific locations based on existing pages. He highlighted the progress made in increasing specific keywords on the website, which will be carried over to the business profile. Ray expressed concern about the inadequacy of their backlinking, to which Clayton responded by showing the detoxification process of their website.

Improving Website's Domain and URL Ratings
Clayton discussed the importance of improving the website's domain rating and URL rating, which are currently at 35 and 21 respectively. He emphasized the need to create more signals to the business profiles and mentioned the ongoing process of cleaning up the website's citations. Corey added that they are also working on a digital PR campaign to further improve the website's visibility. Ray expressed some confusion about the numbers but agreed to the importance of improving the website's ratings.

Consistent Citations for Improved Google Signals
In the meeting, Clayton emphasized the importance of presenting consistent information across all citations to improve Google's correlation of signals about the brand. He also discussed the need to clean up incorrect information in citations. Ray raised a question about competitors potentially using gray hat techniques to increase website clicks, to which Clayton confirmed that such practices exist but are filtered out by Google. Corey revealed that their agency has been using a similar technique for over a decade.

Team Engagement and SEO StrategiesIn the meeting, Ray expressed his desire for the team to be fully engaged and committed to achieving results. He emphasized the importance of trust and confidence in the team's efforts. Clayton provided an overview of the team's current SEO strategies and their impact on the website's performance. Ray and Daniel agreed that they don't need detailed updates on the team's activities, but they want to ensure that the team is working diligently. The team's goal is to deliver results and win, as stated by Ray and Daniel.Planning

Office Locations and Website ChangesIn the meeting, Ray emphasized the importance of planning ahead for future office locations and suggested engaging a social media team to create content for SEO. Clayton proposed changes to the website's main menu and footer, which Daniel agreed to, as long as it improved optimization and case generation. Clayton also mentioned the possibility of sharing the created social media content with them.

Duplicate Pages Website Performance IssueClayton discussed the issue of duplicate pages on their website, which could potentially harm their website's performance. He proposed either keeping the pages live and no-indexing them or removing them altogether. Corey suggested making discretionary changes to the website and informing the partners about the changes. Ray emphasized the importance of considering the website's overall performance and conversion rates when making changes.

Website Overhaul and Digital PR Campaign
In the meeting, Clayton discussed the overhaul of the website's conversion elements and the reorganization of certain pages. Ray expressed concerns about the original website's design and the lack of involvement in its creation. Clayton assured that the overhaul would not affect any conversion elements and would improve the website's appearance. Corey updated the team on the digital PR campaign, which involves ideation, data validation, and pitching to media outlets. Daniel and Corey are leading this campaign, aiming to secure backlinks for the website. The team also discussed the need for a new logo and branding for the firm, with Corey suggesting a contact for this task.
